mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and the Federal Funds Rate is low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy despite inflationary pressures. This can lead to asset price bubbles and subsequent corrections, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: inflationary environment with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive or flat (within +/- 0.5%) when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In a robust economic expansion, increasing industrial production reflects strong corporate activity and demand for goods, which translates quickly into positive market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with S&P 500 5-day returns during periods officially designated as economic expansions, with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates a weakening construction sector and broader economic demand, leading to concerns about future growth and negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat (within +/- 0.5%) following periods where HOUST declines by more than 15% year-over-year for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS month-over-month growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.1) with S&P 500 5-day returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is low and the Federal Funds Rate is rising, it signals a restrictive monetary policy in a weakening economy, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth < 1% AND FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat (within +/- 0.5%) when GDPC1 annual growth is below 1% and FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.25 percentage points in three months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery, strong GDP growth indicates a rebound in economic activity and corporate earnings, which drives investor optimism and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods officially designated as post-recession recovery despite strong GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth.
